name: Covariant Galileon dark energy (quartic)
summary: A scalar-tensor extension of GR in the Horndeski class with non-trivial G_4(X), proposed as a self-accelerating dark-energy alternative to the cosmological constant. The 'covariant Galileon' is the canonical worked example of a modified-gravity model that GW170817 killed: any Horndeski theory with G_4,X != 0 or G_5 != 0 generically predicts c_T^2 != 1 in cosmological backgrounds. Included here as a deliberately-failing test case for the evaluator skeleton.

lineage.citation: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini, 'The Galileon as a local modification of gravity', PRD 79, 064036 (2009)
Ezquiaga and Zumalacarregui, 'Dark Energy After GW170817: Dead Ends and the Road Ahead', PRL 119, 251304 (2017)

alpha_variation_atomic_clocks.note: Galileon coupled conformally to matter (the 'covariant' construction) generically induces a time-dependent effective Newton constant and thus alpha variation; the magnitude depends on the conformal-coupling parameter beta and is sub-model dependent.

antihydrogen_gravity_alpha_g.note: Galileon couples universally to matter via the metric (plus Vainshtein-screened fifth-force corrections that vanish in laboratory environments); a_gbar / g = +1 to high precision.

equivalence_principle_microscope.note: Vainshtein screening drives the scalar fifth-force exponentially small in dense laboratory environments; residual eta predicted to be << 10^-15. Symmetry-breaking corrections at second order in the Vainshtein expansion can be at most ~10^-16 for typical Galileon parameters.
equivalence_principle_microscope.reference: Hiramatsu et al., PRD 87, 063525 (2013); Vainshtein, Phys. Lett. B 39, 393 (1972)

graviton_mass_gw_dispersion.note: Galileon adds a scalar degree of freedom but does not give the graviton a mass; tensor mode remains massless.

graviton_speed_gw170817.note: Quartic covariant Galileon with G_4,X != 0 generically predicts c_T^2 - 1 of order Omega_DE in cosmological backgrounds (~0.1-1). Specific value depends on field configuration at GW170817's redshift. This framework was killed by GW170817 precisely because |c_gw - c|/c = 0.1 is fourteen orders of magnitude above the +/- 10^-15 bound.
graviton_speed_gw170817.reference: Ezquiaga and Zumalacarregui, PRL 119, 251304 (2017), arXiv:1710.05901; Sakstein and Jain, PRL 119, 251303 (2017), arXiv:1710.05893

inverse_square_law_short_distance.note: Vainshtein screening makes Galileon fifth force vanish in high-density regions including the Earth's surface; sub-mm gravity is purely Newtonian. Equivalent to alpha = 0 in the Yukawa parameterization at all sub-mm lambda.
inverse_square_law_short_distance.reference: Vainshtein, PLB 39, 393 (1972); Babichev and Deffayet, Class. Quant. Grav. 30, 184001 (2013)

katrin_neutrino_mass.note: Galileon does not predict neutrino masses; this benchmark is a particle-physics constraint that the framework inherits from its assumed SM matter sector.

proton_decay_lifetime.note: Galileon does not modify the SM matter sector or violate baryon number; proton is stable.
proton_decay_lifetime.reference: Particle Data Group, 'Baryon-Number Violating Processes' review, Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)

sum_neutrino_masses_cosmology.note: Galileon dark energy modifies the cosmological background and perturbations; the Sum m_nu cosmological bound depends on the joint fit including the modified-gravity prediction. Without redoing the Boltzmann integration in CLASS/CAMB this cannot be quoted as a clean number.

strong_cp_theta_qcd.note: Galileon does not address the strong CP problem; theta_QCD remains a free parameter inherited from the SM matter sector.
